meta_description: Custom injection moulding services in the UK. Prototypes and production parts in as fast as 1 day. 100+ materials, free analysis, no MOQ. Get an instant quote.

sections.heading: Online Injection Moulding Services for Custom Plastic Parts
sections.text: Online Injection Moulding Services for Custom Plastic Parts Digital manufacturing for plastic injection moulding prototypes and on-demand production parts in as fast as 1 day. Injection moulding (also known as injection molding in the US) is ideal for producing high-quality plastic parts at scale. Request an instant quote online today. Get Instant Quote Start Production Quote

sections.heading: What is injection moulding?
sections.text: What is injection moulding? Plastic injection moulding is a manufacturing process that produces plastic parts by injecting molten resin into a custom mould. The process is ideal for creating repeatable, high-quality components with tight tolerances, from prototypes through to production quantities.

sections.heading: Injection Moulding Processes
sections.text: Injection Moulding Processes At Protolabs we offer plastic injection moulding, liquid silicone rubber (LSR) moulding, overmoulding, insert moulding, prototyping and on-demand manufacturing to ISO 9001 quality standards . Make the move from prototyping to on-demand manufacturing for affordable, high-quality moulded parts within days, with a free prototyping tool when you switch.

sections.heading: Plastic Injection Moulding
sections.text: Plastic Injection Moulding Plastic injection moulding is a manufacturing process where resin in a barrel is heated to a molten state, then shot into a mould to form a final production-grade thermoplastic part. Lead Time: 1-15 Working Days Learn More

sections.heading: Liquid Silicone Rubber Moulding
sections.text: Liquid Silicone Rubber Moulding Liquid silicone rubber moulding is a thermoset process that mixes a two-component compound together, which is then heat cured in the mould with a platinum catalyst to produce a final part. Lead Time: 1-15 Working Days Learn More

sections.heading: Insert Moulding
sections.text: Insert Moulding Insert moulding is a process where metal or other components are placed into the mould before plastic is injected around them. This creates a single, durable part that integrates both the insert and the moulded material. Lead time: 1–15 working days Learn More

sections.heading: Overmoulding
sections.text: Overmoulding Overmoulding is a two-part injection moulding process in which one material is moulded over a second substrate part. This technique is often used to add ergonomic grips, improve functionality, or enhance the appearance of a component. Lead time: 1–15 working days Learn more

sections.heading: Additional Capabilities for Injection Moulding Orders
sections.text: Additional Capabilities for Injection Moulding Orders

sections.heading: Prototyping and On-demand Production
sections.text: Prototyping and On-demand Production We provide two injection moulding service options. One is best suited for those who need smaller part quantities often associated with prototyping and the other is a good fit for those who require larger part quantities, and is available on-demand with our low-volume production. Learn More

sections.heading: Injection Moulding Finishing
sections.text: Injection Moulding Finishing At Protolabs, we provide a wide selection of post-process options for injection moulding, including threaded inserts, pad printing, laser engraving, basic assembly, and inspection reports. Learn More

sections.heading: Quality Control and Standards
sections.text: Quality Control and Standards We use advanced moulding techniques with a focus on critical dimensions through our automated CMM process. Every order includes comprehensive manufacturing analysis, design consulting, and quality reports like FAIs and capability reports. Learn More

sections.heading: Injection Mould Tooling Options and Quality Reporting
sections.text: Injection Mould Tooling Options and Quality Reporting We offer two services for any stage of your product life cycle. Our prototype and rapid injection moulding service accelerates product development by delivering moulded parts in days, helping teams to validate designs and iterate quickly. For low-volume injection moulding, our On-Demand Manufacturing service enables reliable production with quick part qualification, scientific moulding, in-process CMM inspections with GD&T, and comprehensive post-processing options. Whether you're developing prototypes or scaling production, we help you to move efficiently from prototype to production. Prototyping On-Demand Manufacturing Objective I need...

sections.heading: Injection Moulding Materials
sections.text: Injection Moulding Materials Thermoplastics Thermosets chevron_left chevron_right ABS Acetal Acetal Copolymer Acetal Homopolymer/ Delrin ETPU HDPE LCP LDPE LLDPE Nylon PBT PC/ABS PC/PBT PEEK PEI PET PETG PMMA ( Acrylic , Plexiglas) Polycarbonate Polypropylene PPA PPE/PS PS PSU TPU Standard silicone (30, 40, 50, 60, and 70 durometers) Medical-grade silicone Optical-grade silicone Fluorosilicone (fuel and oil resistant) We have wide selection of more than 100 thermoplastic and thermoset materials. And if you're looking for alternative material options, check out our guide to resin substitutes for ABS, PC, PP, and other commonly moulded plastics. Learn More

sections.heading: Advantages of Injection Moulding
sections.text: Advantages of Injection Moulding Injection moulding is one of the most efficient manufacturing processes for producing high-quality plastic parts at scale. The benefits of injection moulding include low per-part costs at higher volumes, excellent repeatability, and the ability to manufacture complex geometries with consistent quality. The process also offers exceptional surface finishes, low material waste, and access to a wide range of engineering-grade thermoplastics and thermosets. From prototype parts to production quantities, injection moulding provides a reliable and scalable solution.

sections.heading: Why Choose Protolabs for Injection Moulding?
sections.text: Why Choose Protolabs for Injection Moulding? Manufacturing Speed Design for Manufacturability Feedback Consultative Design Service No Minimum Order Quantity (MOQs) Single Supplier European Production Quality Capabilities Reduce your product development cycle by weeks —sometimes months — and bridge to production with injection-moulded parts within days. Our automated quoting, design analysis, and manufacturing process allows us to ship IM orders as fast as 1 day. Every quote includes real-time pricing and design analysis. sections.heading: What's in an Online Injection Moulding Quote?
sections.text: What's in an Online Injection Moulding Quote? We do quoting a little differently. When you request an injection moulding quote, you'll not only receive accurate pricing, but also a manufacturing analysis—within just a few hours. Our online quoting platform also provides you with gate and ejector pin layouts and a price curve tool to help select the best tooling option for your project. Get a Quote
